# Project Opportunity Map — `<project-name>`

> **Companion artifacts:** `project-purpose-and-feature-catalog.md` (feature inventory) · `project-fundamentals-and-design-context.md` (design DNA) · `research-agent-context-pack.md` (researcher briefing)

> **Purpose.** Forward-looking synthesis. Surfaces ideas, expansion paths, and strategic directions grounded in the catalog and fundamentals artifacts. Every opportunity must be evidence-linked and risk-tagged. Do not fabricate.

> **How to read.** Each opportunity carries: rationale (what makes it ripe), evidence link (where in the code/docs the signal lives), strategic value, risk class (`low`/`medium`/`high`), and architectural impact (`none`/`local`/`global`).

## Near-Adjacent Opportunities

Ideas that extend existing functionality without changing the architecture. Cheapest to pursue. High likelihood of fitting the project's character.

| Opportunity | Rationale | Evidence | Strategic value | Risk | Arch impact |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | | |

## Bolder Novel Ideas

Ideas that combine existing capabilities in new ways or address adjacent problems the project doesn't yet serve. Not architectural rewrites — additive but ambitious.

| Opportunity | Rationale | Evidence | Strategic value | Risk | Arch impact |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | | |

## Ideas That Require Architectural Change

Opportunities that cannot be pursued without altering load-bearing parts of the system. Most expensive; require the highest justification.

For each, explicitly state:
- What architectural assumption must change.
- Which existing constraints block it today.
- What migration path is even feasible.

| Opportunity | Required change | Blocked by | Migration feasibility | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | |

## Business Model Extensions

Directions that change how the project creates or captures value. May or may not require code changes — many are purely positioning shifts.

| Direction | What it changes | Required capabilities | Existing assets that help | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | |

## Workflow Automation Opportunities

Internal opportunities: where repeated manual work in the project's own development or operation could be automated. Often low-risk, high-leverage.

| Opportunity | Current pain | Evidence | Effort | Value |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | |

## Research Questions Ranked by Strategic Value

Questions whose answers would unlock or invalidate large amounts of downstream planning. Rank by how much downstream optionality each unlocks.

| Rank | Question | What answering it unlocks | Cost to answer |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | | | |
| 2 | | | |

## Risks of Pursuing Each Direction

A consolidated risk register across all opportunities above. Each entry: which direction triggers it, the failure mode, and a mitigation if known.

| Direction | Failure mode | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| | | | |
